nodes.label: AI as research assistant with verification
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable source without verification
nodes.grounding_span: I always ask for citations so I can just click the links and not rely on the model because it does make mistakes

nodes.label: AI for information gathering vs. legal reasoning
nodes.label_negative: AI for legal reasoning and advice
nodes.grounding_span: When it comes to reasoning, like how could these laws apply to the current situation I am researching for, I use my own reasoning

nodes.label: Trust in AI based on factual accuracy
nodes.label_negative: Blind trust in AI without verification
nodes.grounding_span: I actually learned over time to not trust anything it says 100% when it has to be factual

nodes.label: AI as creative problem-solving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as limited to routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: It actually found a legal angle that I had not considered yet

nodes.label: AI proficiency as a professional skill
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to professional skills
nodes.grounding_span: the best thing I can do in maintaining my skills is learning how to utilize Ai efficiently

nodes.label: AI's role in human-centered legal work
nodes.label_negative: AI replacing human judgment in legal work
nodes.grounding_span: I have to think about the human aspect of it. I have to speak and try to influence others to agree with my stance, which Ai can't do

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: it can save me hours of reading

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as breakthrough tool
nodes.grounding_span: It actually found a legal angle that I had not considered yet. It was amazing!

nodes.label: Caution due to past AI mistakes
nodes.grounding_span: I actually learned over time to not trust anything it says 100% when it has to be factual

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI reliability
nodes.grounding_span: I hope that as the technology develops, it stops making as many mistakes

nodes.label: Skepticism toward colleagues' overtrust
nodes.grounding_span: they trust it too much and make legal mistakes because they don't research it
